# Execution Plan: RQ-0803

## Task Goal

Enable Xcode warnings-as-errors in the CI gate to prevent deprecation warnings from passing silently. This aligns Swift/Xcode build behavior with the existing Rust Clippy behavior where warnings are treated as errors.

## Why This Matters

The repository has a CI contract violation:
- AGENTS.md states: "NO ERRORS OR WARNINGS ARE ACCEPTABLE"
- The Rust side uses `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` (line 44 of Makefile) which fails on warnings
- The Xcode/Swift side has NO equivalent flag, allowing warnings to pass
- This creates a double-standard that violates the repo's quality gate contract
- Example: `CrashReporter.swift:195` contains deprecated `String(cString:)` usage that currently passes CI but should fail

## Changes Required

### 1. Modify Makefile - Add `-warnings-as-errors=YES` to xcodebuild commands

#### Target: `macos-build` (lines 151-164)

Current code:
```makefile
macos-build:
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory macos-preflight
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory build
	@derived_data_path="$(XCODE_DERIVED_DATA_ROOT)/build"; \
	echo "→ macOS build (Xcode build)..."; \
	rm -rf "$$derived_data_path" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	xcodebuild \
		-project apps/RalphMac/RalphMac.xcodeproj \
		-scheme RalphMac \
		-configuration Release \
		-destination 'platform=macOS' \
		-derivedDataPath "$$derived_data_path" \
		CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
		build
```

**Change**: Add `-warnings-as-errors=YES` flag after `CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY=""` and before `build`

New code:
```makefile
macos-build:
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory macos-preflight
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory build
	@derived_data_path="$(XCODE_DERIVED_DATA_ROOT)/build"; \
	echo "→ macOS build (Xcode build)..."; \
	rm -rf "$$derived_data_path" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	xcodebuild \
		-project apps/RalphMac/RalphMac.xcodeproj \
		-scheme RalphMac \
		-configuration Release \
		-destination 'platform=macOS' \
		-derivedDataPath "$$derived_data_path" \
		CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
		-warnings-as-errors=YES \
		build
```

#### Target: `macos-test` (lines 166-189)

Current code:
```makefile
macos-test:
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory macos-preflight
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory build
	@derived_data_path="$(XCODE_DERIVED_DATA_ROOT)/test"; \
	ralph_bin_path="$$(pwd)/target/release/ralph"; \
	include_ui_tests="$(RALPH_UI_TESTS)"; \
	if [ "$$include_ui_tests" = "1" ]; then \
		echo "→ macOS tests (Xcode, including UI tests - will take over mouse/keyboard)..."; \
		skipped_tests=""; \
	else \
		echo "→ macOS tests (Xcode, skipping UI tests - use RALPH_UI_TESTS=1 to include)..."; \
		skipped_tests="-skip-testing RalphMacUITests"; \
	fi; \
	rm -rf "$$derived_data_path" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	RALPH_BIN_PATH="$$ralph_bin_path" \
	xcodebuild \
		-project apps/RalphMac/RalphMac.xcodeproj \
		-scheme RalphMac \
		-configuration Debug \
		-destination 'platform=macOS' \
		-derivedDataPath "$$derived_data_path" \
		CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
		$$skipped_tests \
		test
```

**Change**: Add `-warnings-as-errors=YES` flag after `CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY=""` and before `$$skipped_tests`

New code:
```makefile
macos-test:
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory macos-preflight
	@$(MAKE) --no-print-directory build
	@derived_data_path="$(XCODE_DERIVED_DATA_ROOT)/test"; \
	ralph_bin_path="$$(pwd)/target/release/ralph"; \
	include_ui_tests="$(RALPH_UI_TESTS)"; \
	if [ "$$include_ui_tests" = "1" ]; then \
		echo "→ macOS tests (Xcode, including UI tests - will take over mouse/keyboard)..."; \
		skipped_tests=""; \
	else \
		echo "→ macOS tests (Xcode, skipping UI tests - use RALPH_UI_TESTS=1 to include)..."; \
		skipped_tests="-skip-testing RalphMacUITests"; \
	fi; \
	rm -rf "$$derived_data_path" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	RALPH_BIN_PATH="$$ralph_bin_path" \
	xcodebuild \
		-project apps/RalphMac/RalphMac.xcodeproj \
		-scheme RalphMac \
		-configuration Debug \
		-destination 'platform=macOS' \
		-derivedDataPath "$$derived_data_path" \
		CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO CODE_SIGN_IDENTITY="" \
		-warnings-as-errors=YES \
		$$skipped_tests \
		test
```

## Verification Steps

1. After making the changes, run `make macos-ci` to verify the CI gate now fails on existing warnings
2. The build should fail due to the deprecation warning in `CrashReporter.swift:195` (using deprecated `String(cString:)`)
3. This is EXPECTED - the CI gate should fail until the actual warnings are fixed (which is task RQ-0804)
4. Confirm the error message clearly indicates it's a warning being treated as an error

## Important Notes

- This task BLOCKS RQ-0804 - the CI gate must fail first before we fix the actual warnings
- Do NOT fix the deprecation warnings in this task - only add the flag
- The `-warnings-as-errors=YES` flag is supported in Xcode 15+ (current minimum)
- This change enforces parity between Rust (Clippy -D warnings) and Swift/Xcode builds
- After this change, ANY warning in the macOS app code will cause CI to fail

## Rollback Plan

If this causes unexpected issues, simply remove the `-warnings-as-errors=YES` lines from both xcodebuild commands in the Makefile.

## Files to Modify

- `Makefile`: Add `-warnings-as-errors=YES` to `macos-build` and `macos-test` targets

## Files to Review (for context only)

- `apps/RalphMac/RalphMac/CrashReporter.swift:195` - Contains the deprecated `String(cString:)` usage that will be blocked
